codeReview.summary: The fix adds dedicated browser/CLI launch-service tests, but it does not prove rapid-switch active-profile routing and does not fully resolve the original failed feature’s blocking gaps.

issues.description: The test claims a rapid switch from chrome1->chrome2, but no active-profile switch occurs; it only sleeps and then calls launchBrowser(for: chrome2.id). This validates direct ID lookup, not final committed active-profile routing after a switch chain.

The CLI rapid-switch test similarly performs no switch operation and asserts by launching with an explicit profile ID (launchCLI(for: claudeProfile.id)). This does not exercise launch routing from committed active state after rapid switching.

The original failed feature’s CTA/log-capture blockers remain unaddressed by this fix commit (which only edits launch-service test files). The original diff still relied on static CTA presence checks and synthetic redaction strings rather than actionable CTA routing and runtime startup/sync log capture evidence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked followedProcedure=true, but required validation-stack steps in the skill were not run/documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md lines 28-33 require swift test + app test + browser/CLI smoke + evidence collection; handoff commandsRun only lists scoped xcodebuild plus swiftc parse.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill requires context-read evidence when followedProcedure=true, but the handoff did not include any context-read command evidence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md line 101 onward defines Context Read Evidence requirement; fix handoff has followedProcedure=true and no context-read commands in verification.commandsRun.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture expects launch actions to use current active profile, but test guidance does not explicitly force proving active-state-to-launch routing (vs explicit profile-id invocation), leading to superficial launch-chain evidence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md Data Flow step 5 says launch uses currently active profile; new tests call launchBrowser/launchCLI with explicit profile IDs (SwitcherBrowserLaunchTests.swift:728, SwitcherCLILaunchTests.swift:983).

summary: Fail: the fix improves launch-service coverage but does not actually verify rapid-switch final active-profile routing, and it does not comprehensively resolve the prior feature’s blocking CTA/log-evidence gaps.
